cycle_context: R9 re-inspection. Prior close (close-report-20260525-050824.md) was CLOSE:NO solely due to bare python3 at test-lifecycle-cas.sh:151,194. Those lines were fixed in task 20260525-095245 (commit d7061447). This report audits the current file state.

violations.finding: Hardcoded flock timeout: `flock -w 10` with error message `within 10s`. Standard 2 explicitly lists timeouts as a category requiring parameterization. No environment variable or CLI parameter override is provided.
violations.recommendation: Add `LOCK_TIMEOUT="${LOCK_TIMEOUT:-10}"` near top of script; replace `flock -w 10` with `flock -w "${LOCK_TIMEOUT}"` and update the error message to `within ${LOCK_TIMEOUT}s`.

violations.finding: Hardcoded flock timeout: `flock -s -w 10` with error message `within 10s`. Standard 2 explicitly lists timeouts as a category requiring parameterization. No environment variable or CLI parameter override is provided.
violations.recommendation: Add `LOCK_TIMEOUT="${LOCK_TIMEOUT:-10}"` near top of script; replace `flock -s -w 10` with `flock -s -w "${LOCK_TIMEOUT}"` and update the error message to `within ${LOCK_TIMEOUT}s`.

prior_blocking_violations_status.use-source-venv at test-lifecycle-cas.sh: FIXED — current file has `( source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python3 - "$FAKE_SCORES" <<'PYEOF'` at line 151. Prior close-report was CLOSE:NO for this; fix confirmed in commit d7061447.
FIXED — current file has `( source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate && python3 - "$FAKE_SCORES" <<'PYEOF'` at line 195 (renumbered). Fix confirmed in commit d7061447.

observations_only.note: Standard 2 — generated pytest files (tests/generated/20260525-050824/test_AC*.py and tests/generated/20260522-000000/test_AC4_7d4395a2a5e37239.py) all hardcode repo = "/dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude". This is an established pattern in this project's generated test framework (prior cycle 20260522-000000 uses same pattern). Standard 2 formally targets scripts/ files; tests/generated/ is outside formal Standard 2 blocking scope per prior close precedent. Advisory portability debt for future cycles: use `from pathlib import Path; REPO = str(Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3])` instead.

standards_passed: no-inline-code (Standard 1 — not applicable; changed files are scripts and pytest files, not .md command/agent files)

use-source-venv (Standard 3 — all python3 calls in shell scripts are preceded by 'source ~/.claude/venv/bin/activate &&' on the same line; test-lifecycle-cas.sh lines 86, 151, 195 all have venv activation; prior violations at :151/:194 confirmed fixed in current file state)
integer-step-numbering (Standard 4 — no decimal or lettered step numbering found in any changed file)
meaningful-naming (Standard 5 — all filenames are descriptive and functional; no forbidden patterns found)

merge-similar-scripts (Standard 7 — score-update.sh appends events, score-inject.sh reads and emits injection block, lifecycle-baseline-import.sh is one-time migration; distinct purposes, no overlap warranting merge)
concise-documentation (Standard 8 — not applicable; changed files are scripts and pytest files, not .md command/agent files)

dangling-references (Standard 10 — all scripts referenced in test files exist on disk; test_AC6 references tests/generated/20260522-000000/test_AC4_7d4395a2a5e37239.py which exists; imports in hooks/doc_sync files resolve correctly)
merge-conflicts (Standard 11 — no merge conflict markers found in any changed file)

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex identified one missed critical finding (hardcoded flock timeout `-w 10` in three scripts) as MISSED CRITICAL with a concrete PROPOSED_FIX. The generated test files' hardcoded repo path was classified OBSERVATION_ONLY. No other missed violations were identified. Standard 3 violations at test-lifecycle-cas.sh:151/194 from the prior close were confirmed fixed in the current file state.

findings.issue: MISSED CRITICAL — Standard 2/9: hardcoded, non-parameterized lock timeout at scripts/score-update.sh:76-77, scripts/score-inject.sh:64-65, and scripts/lifecycle-baseline-import.sh:52-53. `flock -w 10` and `within 10s` are hardcoded timeouts; Standard 2 explicitly includes timeouts as a category requiring parameterization.
findings.proposed_fix: Add `LOCK_TIMEOUT="${LOCK_TIMEOUT:-10}"` or parse `--lock-timeout <seconds>` in each script; use `flock -w "${LOCK_TIMEOUT}"` / `flock -s -w "${LOCK_TIMEOUT}"`, and change stderr to `within ${LOCK_TIMEOUT}s`.
findings.rationale: Finding is correct and in scope. Standard 2 explicitly includes timeouts as requiring parameterization. Three violations added to violations[] with introduced_in_diff:true and blocking_for_close:true.

findings.issue: OBSERVATION_ONLY — generated pytest files hardcode `repo = "/dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude"`. Prior precedent may keep this outside formal Standard 2 blocking scope for generated tests.
findings.proposed_fix: Replace with `repo = str(Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3])` after importing `Path` from `pathlib`, or allow an env override.
findings.rationale: Standard 2 formally targets scripts/ files; tests/generated/ is outside formal Standard 2 blocking scope per prior close precedent confirmed by Codex. Logged in observations_only[] as advisory portability debt for future cycles.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Three violations added for hardcoded flock timeout (Standard 2) in score-update.sh:76-77, score-inject.sh:64-65, and lifecycle-baseline-import.sh:52-53. Hardcoded repo path in generated tests kept as observation_only per Codex scope analysis and prior close precedent. Pre-existing english-only violations retained from prior report with introduced_in_diff:false / advisory status.
